A flamboyant entrepreneur who made toys used by millions of children around the world for 25 years took his own life when the business began to go under. David Strang, 52, made toys including supersized bouncy balls and indoor boomerangs that were sold at outlets including Hamleys, Walmart, Sports Direct and Amazon. Strang also excelled as a gatecrasher, sneaking his way into events like the Oscars, the Baftas and the Brits - and over the years took selfies with the likes of Halle Berry, Kate Hudson, Tom Jones, Dizzee Rascal, Anne-Marie and Steven Tyler.

The Prince of Wales has shared a never-before-seen photo of the late Princess Diana in a poignant Mother's Day message. The picture shows Diana - who would have been 65 this year - with a two-year-old Prince William in a field of flowers at Highgrove in 1984. It is from the Prince's private family collection and has not been shown to the public before. The heartfelt caption reads: 'Remembering my mother, today and every day.'

  • John was very sick for a long time before he died. She would've known that the end was coming a long while ago; the ups and downs of a long-term illness are hard on a relationship. In the late‑'80s, Kelly McGillis was Hollywood's golden girl - the actress everyone wanted, the next big Oscar contender, and the glam bombshell lighting up every red carpet.  From igniting sparks in blockbuster Top Gun to gripping audiences in the thriller Witness and the intense drama The Accused, she proved she could do it all on the big screen. She shared sizzling chemistry with heavyweights like Tom Cruise and Harrison Ford - smoldering as their love interest - and held her own opposite the legendary Kurt Russell in Winter People and alongside John Goodman in The Babe.

A line of tourists touch up their makeup before strutting across a rooftop in Rio de Janeiro's largest favela, posing for a drone as it zooms out to show dramatic aerial views of the hillside community. Set to an infectious beat, the video of the Rocinha favela has exploded on social media at a time when the city is drawing record tourist numbers. It has also created a sense of unease, with dozens of comments accusing visitors of glamourising poverty and crime in a low-income community dominated by drug traffickers.

It's difficult when you've been raised by narcissistic parents, the only way to get by is to become a pleaser. So it's ingrained from childhood and you're very sensitive to displeasure from people. It pains you. Only recently started being assertive with this, I'm 52. I've had enough of people mistreating me as that's what happens. Funnily enough if someone's kind I treat them well in return. But most people don't think that way. Have to protect yourself, try to start it younger than I did. Save yourself a lot of pain and regret.

Just another so called bankrupt that has lots of money.

As legendary jockey Frankie Dettori embarks upon the next chapter of his life and faces the threat of jail time, it is worth a closer look at his extraordinary story so far. The 55-year-old leapt from his horse in victory for the final time last month in Brazil - bringing down the curtain on a wildly successful 35-year career. Ever the entertainer, the charismatic Italian's life away from the track has been colourful to say the least and he's made plenty of headlines over decades in the public eye, often for the wrong reasons.
